meaning: v.使中断,使分裂,使瓦解,使陷于混乱,破坏
examples: A stroke can disrupt the supply of oxygen to the brain.
It could disrupt the food chains there.
They can disrupt experiments with the bacteria organisms they carry.
The strike will now disrupt students as it coincides with GCSE and A-Level examinations.
We will continue to be the influencing player and disrupt existing cozy arrangements, vows Rajiv.
